author: mai

findings.detail: useReducedMotion hook correctly respects prefers-reduced-motion media query with change listener. All animation components (PageTransition, FadeIn, AnimatedList, SlideIn) short-circuit to plain <div> when reduced=true. Excellent WCAG 2.1 Motion compliance.

useReducedMotion initializes with reduced=false and sets real value in useEffect — causes brief animation flash on initial render for users with reduced-motion preference. Recommend initializing from matchMedia synchronously via useState initializer.

v149 contrast fixes: PLATFORM label now text-slate-500 dark:text-slate-300 + font-semibold (WCAG AA). Sidebar section headers text-slate-500 dark:text-slate-300 + font-bold. SummaryCard borders border-gray-300 dark:border-gray-600 for better visibility. All applied correctly.

badge.tsx success/warning variants use bg-emerald-950/60 and bg-amber-950/60 without dark: prefix — dark-only colors that appear nearly black in light mode. Destructive variant correctly uses dark: modifiers. Needs light-mode variants: bg-emerald-50 text-emerald-700 / dark:bg-emerald-950/60 dark:text-emerald-300.
Badge default variant uses bg-zinc-50 text-zinc-900 with no dark: override. Low impact if default is rarely used in dark contexts.

Animation constants well-organized as single source of truth. Durations 0.15-0.3s are professional. Page transitions use subtle y:6→0 with opacity. cardHover scale:1.01 and cardTap scale:0.995 micro-interactions are appropriately subtle for a dashboard.

ProviderLogo uses colored pill backgrounds (12% opacity) for all 11 providers — unified visual language. AWS uses logos:aws, kubeconfig uses mdi:file-cog-outline (gray, distinct from k8s blue). Good differentiation.
ConnectionStatus real-time stats (Total Pods, CPU, Alerts) in TopBar have no aria-live region — screen readers miss updates. Consider aria-live='polite' on dynamically changing stat values.

Only 2 test files found in apps/web (ai-keys tests). No component unit tests for UI components modified in v148-v149 polish phase. Animation behavior, badge variants, and ProviderLogo icon selection have zero unit test coverage. E2E covers integration but not component regression.

E2E v149: 86/96 passed, 0 failures, 10 skipped. verdict=PASS. Selector fix (getByRole for dismiss button) and AnimatePresence timeout bump to 15s correctly resolved E2E flakiness. Good selector hygiene.

Codebase well-structured: animations isolated in /components/animations and /lib/animation-constants. Consistent CSS custom properties (var(--color-*)) for theming. TypeScript types present on all component props reviewed.

notes: Phase-g-visual-polish review. v148-v149 commits show systematic, high-quality UI work — 10+ polish items addressed across contrast, icons, badges, animations, and sidebar. Accessibility is a strength (reduced-motion, aria-labels, 44px touch targets). Main concerns: success/warning badge light-mode styling gap (medium) and test coverage debt for new UI components (medium). E2E gate: 0 failures, PASS. Score 8.7 → PASS.
